scope: hexa-runner Docker image subsystem (docker/runner/Dockerfile + tool/build_runner_image.hexa + build_interp_linux.hexa + build_hexa_cli_linux.hexa) — the single Linux landing pad for every Mac-side hexa exec per ~/.hx/bin/hexa hard-landing policy (post-2026-04-25 jetsam SIGKILL incident).
approach: raw#73 read-only audit, $0; no rebuilds, no git/docker mutations

image_inventory.running_container: hexa-exec (long-lived; sleep infinity; reused by `~/.hx/bin/hexa` via `docker exec`)

image_inspect.note: Inspect returns compact size; `docker images` reports 236 MB virtual (incl. base layer not deduped at-rest)

layer_breakdown.layer: RUN apt install ca-certificates perl tini curl wget

size_and_layers.wasted_payload: x86_64 ELFs (~2.6 MB) are COPY'd then `rm -f` after arch-select; they still live in the buildkit cache layer but not the final image. The COPY-then-discard wastes layer bytes vs per-arch COPY-with-buildx-platform-branch.

rationale.ca-certificates: TLS root store for fetch tools
rationale.perl: provides /usr/bin/shasum used by main.hexa AOT cache + source fingerprinting
rationale.tini: init-aware PID 1 — required for cmd_run() watcher subshell PPID==1 orphan-detection (without it, exit 137 SIGKILL before stdout flush)
rationale.curl_wget: added 2026-04-26; nexus bridges (oeis/gw/arxiv/simbad/wikipedia/openalex/uniprot) exec(curl/wget) for HTTPS fetches — 7/16 bridges FAIL'd before this add

transitive_chain_examples: libcurl4 + libnghttp2-14 + libssh2-1 + libsasl2-2 + libgssapi-krb5-2 + libbrotli1 + librtmp1 (curl deps)

stages.name: cache-only multi-arch buildx
stages.purpose: verify both arches compile under Dockerfile

stages.name: host-arch single-platform --load
stages.purpose: make image visible to local `docker images` (buildx --load can only materialize 1 arch)

rebuild_pipeline.buildx_builder: hexa-runner-builder (docker-container driver — required for multi-arch; default driver cannot do multi-platform)
rebuild_pipeline.interp_compile_cost_hint: build_interp_linux.hexa header notes ~150s for arm64 with -O2; uses 600s `timeout --kill-after=10`
rebuild_pipeline.cli_compile_cost_hint: smaller (~575 KB ELF, simpler dispatch); no explicit timeout note
rebuild_pipeline.cold_full_rebuild_estimate_s: ~330+ (interp arm64 ~150s + interp x86_64 ~150s via qemu emulation under buildx + cli ~30s + apt layer ~15s + buildx fan-out)
rebuild_pipeline.warm_rebuild_estimate_s: ~10-30 (buildkit caches everything except changed COPY layers)
rebuild_pipeline.dockerignore_hygiene: tight allowlist — only build/hexa_{linux,interp_linux}_{arm64,x86_64} + docker/ enter context; .git excluded explicitly

exec_profile.nexus_bridge_pattern: host: -v $HOME/core/nexus:/nexus:ro -e NEXUS_HOME=/nexus (resolver should add when omega/drill/canon issued)
exec_profile.container_reuse_strategy: hexa-exec persistent worker, sleep infinity, reused via `docker exec` (raw#44)

physics_or_limits.buildx_load_unicity: buildx --load can only materialize ONE platform into the local engine — multi-arch verification requires a second cache-only build pass (current pipeline does both)
physics_or_limits.qemu_cross_emulation_tax: amd64 builds on arm64 host run via qemu-user — interp compile (~150s native) becomes minutes under emulation
physics_or_limits.static_musl_floor: interp ELF 1.69 MB + cli ELF 575 KB = 2.27 MB binary floor; can't be smaller without splitting interp into modules
physics_or_limits.debian_slim_floor: 108 MB base — alpine could cut to ~5 MB but musl-static binaries don't need libc, so a `FROM scratch` or `FROM gcr.io/distroless/static` variant is theoretically viable
physics_or_limits.apt_fan_out_cost: 5 deliberate packages pull 70-ish transitive deps (libperl, libcurl, krb5, gnutls, ldap, sasl, ...) — explains the 72.5 MB apt layer despite minimal name list
physics_or_limits.shasum_dependency: perl pulled solely for /usr/bin/shasum; replacing with sha256sum from coreutils (already present) would let perl + libperl5.36 + perl-modules-5.36 (~50 MB combined) be dropped
physics_or_limits.tini_pid1_invariant: cmd_run() watcher subshell uses [ $PPID -eq 1 ] for orphan detection — non-negotiable; cannot drop tini without rewriting watchdog
physics_or_limits.raw73_admissibility: minimum-add-only — the curl/wget add was justified by 7/16 bridge FAILs; no preemptive package additions

friction_observed.observation: 236 MB final image; 108 MB is debian-slim base, 72.5 MB is apt layer (60% of which is libcurl+libperl transitive). Binary payload is only ~2.3 MB.
friction_observed.leverage: FROM scratch / FROM gcr.io/distroless/static would drop ~175 MB; replace shasum-via-perl with sha256sum-via-coreutils to drop ~50 MB while keeping debian-slim

friction_observed.observation: Cold full rebuild ~5+ min (qemu-emulated x86_64 interp dominates). Default pipeline ALWAYS builds both arches in cache, even when host-only iteration is the goal.
friction_observed.leverage: expose --host-only flag in build_runner_image.hexa to skip the multi-arch cache pass during dev iteration

friction_observed.observation: First container exec ~5s; warm ~50ms. Compounds because EVERY non-carve-out Mac hexa exec routes through docker.
friction_observed.leverage: already mitigated by persistent hexa-exec worker; verify the 50ms warm ceiling actually holds via benchmark

friction_observed.observation: Dockerfile COPYs both arch ELFs unconditionally then `rm -f` the wrong-arch pair in the install RUN. The COPY layers (4.9 MB combined) are wasted on per-arch images.
friction_observed.leverage: use buildx TARGETARCH-conditional COPY (--from with ARG branching) so only the host-arch ELFs enter the image

friction_observed.observation: Image is intentionally minimal: no gdb, strace, ps, top, less. When workloads SIGKILL/segfault inside the container, root-cause requires either rebuilding with debug or `docker cp`-ing artifacts out.
friction_observed.leverage: ship a parallel hexa-runner-debug:latest variant with strace+gdb+procps; resolver opts in via `HEXA_DEBUG_RUNNER=1`

friction_observed.observation: raw#44 hard-landing policy is justified by cgroup CPU/RAM caps, but the image has no cgroup tooling — workloads can't see their own limits, can't self-throttle, can't emit /sys/fs/cgroup observability.
friction_observed.leverage: add a `hexa runtime status` subcommand that reads /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.max, memory.max, memory.current; no apt add needed

friction_observed.observation: 5 hexa-runner tags currently in local registry (latest, ab8bec13, 5bd9f630, e075b3e8, 87916b4c) consuming combined ~1 GB virtual. No GC policy.
friction_observed.leverage: add `tool/runner_image_gc.hexa` to keep last-N shortsha tags + always-keep latest

next_cycles: omega-runner-1: distroless / FROM scratch trial — cut image to <10 MB, validate hexa run + bridge fetches still work
omega-runner-2: shasum→sha256sum migration in main.hexa AOT cache + drop perl from apt (~50 MB save, no behavior change)
omega-runner-3: TARGETARCH-conditional COPY refactor — eliminate 4.9 MB wrong-arch COPY layer waste
omega-runner-4: warm-exec benchmark — confirm raw44 50ms ceiling holds across N=1000 invocations; quantify drift
omega-runner-5: hexa-runner-debug:latest sister image (gdb/strace/procps) + resolver HEXA_DEBUG_RUNNER opt-in
omega-runner-6: cgroup self-introspection — `hexa runtime status` reads /sys/fs/cgroup/{cpu.max,memory.max,memory.current}
omega-runner-7: tag GC policy + image registry hygiene tool
